svg.text: SPANISH
HISTORY & IBERIAN ROMANCE
SPANISH ORIGINS
Latin brought to Iberia by Roman conquest (218 BCE)
Vulgar Latin + Visigothic influence → Ibero-Romance
711 CE: Moorish conquest; Arabic influence begins (800+ loanwords)
El Cantar de Mio Cid (~1200): earliest major Castilian text
1492: Reconquista complete; Nebrija publishes first Spanish grammar
Same year: Columbus sets sail; Spanish becomes global language
SPANISH TODAY
~500M native speakers; 2nd most spoken language by natives
Official in 20 countries; UN official language
Cervantes: Don Quijote (1605) — first modern novel
CATALAN HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
CATALAN ORIGINS
9th c.: Catalan emerges in the Marca Hispanica (Carolingian)
County of Barcelona: independent from both France and Castile
13th c.: Crown of Aragon expands; Catalan becomes empire language
Catalan spoken in: Sicily, Sardinia, Greece under Aragon rule
GOLDEN AGE TO SUPPRESSION
Ramon Llull (1232-1316): first major Catalan prose author
Joanot Martorell: Tirant lo Blanc (1490) — early European novel
1716: Nueva Planta decree — Castilian replaces Catalan officially
Renaixenca (19th c.): Catalan literary and cultural revival
1936-75: Franco dictatorship bans Catalan in public life
CATALAN TODAY
~10M speakers; co-official in Catalonia, Valencia, Balearics
Andorra: Catalan is the SOLE official language
Also spoken in Alghero (Sardinia) and Roussillon (France)
Independence debate (2017 referendum): language as identity
ROMANCE SIBLINGS
Both: descended from Vulgar Latin of Iberian Peninsula
Both: influenced by Arabic during Moorish period
Spanish: expanded south and west • Catalan: expanded east and south
Catalan: closer to Occitan/French • Spanish: closer to Portuguese
Two Romance languages, one Iberian history, distinct identities
PLATE 26d ALT · SPANISH / CATALAN · HISTORY
